findings.evidence: In the 'SUPPORTING DOCUMENTATION' section of the PDF, items are preceded by empty square checkboxes. In the HTML, this is rendered as a standard unordered list with bullet points.
The PDF features side-by-side lines for signatures, such as a line for 'Signature' and a parallel line for 'Print Name'. The HTML collapses these onto single lines and merges the labels beneath them.

On the PDF, sections intended for longer textual input (e.g., 'Strengths:', 'Interests:', 'Triggers:') are contained within large, bordered rectangular boxes. The HTML renders these simply as text labels with white space, losing the visual bounding container.

findings.description: Merged table cells split into repeating rows
findings.evidence: The 'Contact Information' table in the PDF uses a single spanned cell in the left column for each role (e.g., 'Mental Health Professional' spans 5 rows). The HTML splits this merged cell and repeats the role name on every individual row.
